evals.prompt: Write unit tests for the OrderDiscountService in our C# Azure Functions project. Requirement: a 10% loyalty discount applies only to members, and orders past the daily cutoff are rejected.
evals.expected_output: xUnit test class with NSubstitute mocks and FluentAssertions, one test per requirement plus edge cases, AAA structure, descriptive Should_..._When_... names, and a requirement->test mapping.
expectations: Detects the C# stack and uses xUnit (per detect_test_framework.py / csharp-xunit.md), not another framework
Generates a test for the member-only loyalty discount and a test for the past-cutoff rejection, each traceable to the stated requirement
Tests follow AAA and Should_<behavior>_When_<condition> naming and mock dependencies at the boundary, not internal collaborators
Determinism is preserved (clock/IDs injected rather than DateTime.Now/Guid.NewGuid in assertions)

evals.prompt: We're about to refactor a legacy React utility formatInvoice() that has no tests. Protect it so the refactor can't change behavior.
evals.expected_output: Characterization tests that pin the current output of formatInvoice() across representative inputs, labeled as capturing current (not necessarily correct) behavior, using the project's detected runner.
expectations: Chooses the legacy/characterization strategy (per legacy-characterization.md), capturing current behavior as the baseline
Tests are explicitly labeled as characterization (pinning current behavior, not verifying a requirement)
Uses the detected runner (Vitest or Jest) and does not modify the source function while adding tests

evals.prompt: Generate the unit tests for the CartSummary React component from this spec while another dev implements it: it shows item count, subtotal, and an empty-cart message. The component file doesn't exist yet.
evals.expected_output: Spec-first React Testing Library tests derived from the acceptance criteria, expected to be red until the component is implemented, querying via accessible roles and asserting each spec behavior.
expectations: Derives tests from the spec/acceptance criteria rather than from non-existent source code (spec-first/parallel mode)
Uses React Testing Library accessible queries (getByRole/getByText) and userEvent, per react-vitest-jest.md
Acknowledges the tests are expected to fail/not-compile until implementation lands, and does not create or modify the source component

non_goals: Generating end-to-end / Playwright browser tests (handled by qa-engineer e2e phase + browser-skill)
Modifying production source code (only test files, test config, and reports)
Writing integration tests beyond the unit/component boundary
